headline: Expense Submissions +58%, Exceptions Halved — PAY Cycle Crunch Ahead
summary: Week of Apr 24–30: Atlas-HXM shows its strongest expense submission week this quarter — addExpense_upserted up 58% and addExpenseLine_upserted up 80% WoW, signaling the AI expense extraction feature is gaining real traction. Exceptions collapsed 52% (2,332 vs 4,835) — the best error health in 6 weeks. SSO success rate holds at 93%. On the risk side: PAY cycle 26 closes in 2 days with only 20% done and MFA shipping without a QA regression test assigned. Rageclicks ticked up 16%, suggesting a new friction point to investigate.
weekSignalReason: Strong product signals (expenses, error health) but PAY cycle at critical risk and rageclicks rising.
execSignal: AMBER — product metrics improving but PAY delivery risk requires immediate triage before May 2 cycle close.
p1Actions: PAY cycle 26 closes May 2 with 80% of scope undone — run scope triage today: what ships, what carries over, what drops.
Assign PAY-1287 (MFA QA regression test) before MFA ships — auth flow breakage would affect all WSE logins.
Add PostHog observability to GTN PROD rollout (FNM2-983) before it goes live — zero telemetry on the primary FNM 2 delivery.
Confirm FNM1-815/807 (Megagen Implantt Sage export blocker) is resolved — customer is blocked from exporting invoices.

regressions.hypothesis: WFM 1 shipped census verification changes (WFM1-1112) — possible flow disruption to task save. Monitor whether the new flagged-field architecture changed the save trigger path.

topRisks.title: PAY cycle 26 ends May 2 at 20% completion
topRisks.reason: Scope grew from 44 to 66 issues mid-cycle. MFA, tokenization, and SQL migration all in flight simultaneously. Current trajectory suggests major carry-over.
topRisks.recommendedAction: Run scope triage immediately — cut to MFA + critical SQL migration only, defer tokenization to cycle 27.

topRisks.title: Rageclicks up 16% (1,603 vs 1,381)
topRisks.reason: Rageclick volume is rising despite lower overall pageviews. A new friction point was introduced this week — likely in an area without strong PostHog coverage (FNM or PAY flows).
topRisks.recommendedAction: Pull a rageclick session recording sample to identify the affected component before next week.

topRisks.title: SSO login failures up 19% (156 vs 131)
topRisks.reason: Despite 93% overall SSO success rate, raw failure count increased. MFA refactor (PAY-1093) shipped this week — confirm it didn't introduce edge-case failures for certain country codes.
topRisks.recommendedAction: Check sso_login_failed breakdown by error type in PostHog against last week's baseline.

instrumentationGaps: FNM2-983 (GTN Acceleration to PROD) — zero PostHog events for GTN mapping or recon approval. Blind to production behavior.
FNM1-653 (Virtual Expense Cards epic) — PR 215 merged, card transactions flow shipping soon, no tracking events defined.
WFM1-1082 (Census verification audit hooks) — in progress but PostHog event for correction completion rate not yet wired.

opportunities.idea: Expense AI extraction is working — addExpense_aiFeedbackBannerShown up 52% to 1,376 shows massive adoption. Build a feedback loop dashboard before competitors catch up.
Census verification comment threads (WFM1-1112) shipped — track flagged field correction completion rate in PostHog to measure workflow improvement.
opportunities.fromPR: WFM1-1112: Re-architecture of assignee flow + comments

opportunities.idea: MFA phone setup up 122% (20 vs 9) — strong early signal. Define target success metric before QA regression test ships.
